file_name: GWG HOLDINGS, INC. - ORDERLY MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 The Seller Trusts and GWG intend to retain one or more nationally recognized bulge bracket investment banks (the "Bank") for the orderly marketing and resale of Shares pursuant to a separate engagement letter (the "Engagement Letter") to advise them in connection with the sale of the Tranches (together, the "Offerings"). Such Engagement Letter shall include customary representations, warranties, covenants and indemnification provisions. The services to be performed by the Bank shall be set forth in such Engagement Letter and are expected to include, among others: (a) assisting in the drafting and preparation of one or more prospectus supplements describing GWG, the Shares and the terms of the Offerings; (b) advising the Seller Trusts on a marketing and distribution strategy for each Tranche of Shares, including whether a particular Tranche should be sold through a block trade, overnight bookbuild, or similar transaction; (c) assisting GWG in preparing marketing materials and conducting one or more "roadshows" and meetings with potential purchasers of the Shares; (d) advising the Seller Trusts as to the timing, structure and pricing of the Offerings; (e) providing other advisory services as are customary for similar transactions. The Engagement Letter shall include a requirement that, prior to any distribution of Stock by the Bank as contemplated by this Agreement, the Bank shall consult with each of GWG and the Trust Advisors as to the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of the respective Tranche.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 The Seller Trusts and GWG intend to retain one or more nationally recognized bulge bracket investment banks (the "Bank") for the marketing and resale of Shares pursuant to a separate agreement (the "Engagement Letter") to advise them in connection with the sale of the Tranches (together, the "Offerings"). The services to be performed by the advisory firm will be outlined in a separate document. The advisory firm will provide advice as needed.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text removes the specific requirements for the Engagement Letter, such as customary representations, warranties, covenants, and indemnification provisions. It also omits the detailed list of services the Bank is expected to perform, such as assisting in drafting prospectus supplements, advising on marketing strategies, assisting with marketing materials and roadshows, and advising on the timing, structure, and pricing of the Offerings. This omission creates uncertainty about the scope of the advisory firm's responsibilities, and creates an in-text contradiction when cross-referenced in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2. Compensation. The Engagement Letter shall provide that the Bank shall not be entitled to any compensation for its advice hereunder or reimbursement of its expenses in connection with this OMA and shall only be entitled to compensation in connection with an Offering as provided in the Engagement Letter and related Distribution Agreement with respect to such Offering.
perturbation.justification: The original text (1.4) specifies that the Engagement Letter will outline the Bank's compensation and reimbursement details. However, the changed text omits the requirement for the Engagement Letter to contain these details, stating only that the advisory firm will 'provide advice as needed.' This contradicts Section 2, which explicitly states that 'The Engagement Letter shall provide that the Bank shall not be entitled to any compensation for its advice hereunder or reimbursement of its expenses...' Without the Engagement Letter defining the Bank's compensation as mandated in Section 2, there is now a direct contradiction regarding how the Bank will be compensated.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts shall offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall be entitled to appoint, in its discretion, an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution. The Seller Trusts further agree that in the event the Bank accepts such role it will be paid customary fees for the performance of its services in connection with such transactions and that such engagement will involve the execution of a standard form agreement with respect to the distribution of each Tranche, which may be in the form of a placement agency agreement, underwriting agreement or other appropriate agreement (each, a "Distribution Agreement"); provided, however, that nothing contained in this OMA or Engagement Letter shall require the Bank to underwrite or purchase all or any portion of a Tranche of Stock for its own account. Notwithstanding the foregoing, it is understood and agreed that the Bank or its affiliates may, solely at its discretion and without any obligation to do so, purchase Stock in any Tranche as principal.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts may offer the advisory firm the right to participate in the distribution. GWG may appoint an additional participant in the distribution. The Seller Trusts further agree that the advisory firm will be paid fees for its services in connection with such transactions; however, nothing contained in this OMA or Engagement Letter shall require the firm to underwrite or purchase any portion of a Tranche of Stock. The firm may purchase Stock in any Tranche.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the specific role of "lead left joint-book-running manager" and the requirement for a "best efforts distribution." It also removes the condition that the advisory firm's engagement will involve the execution of a standard form agreement, such as a placement agency agreement or underwriting agreement. The removal of these specific terms creates uncertainty about the structure of the distribution and how the advisory firm will be engaged, contradicting sections that depend on these structures being in place. Particularly, it removes references to the Bank and replace it with advisory firm, but some parts of section 1.6 are being reffered with Bank, which creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 Each such Distribution Agreement shall set out the customary terms and conditions for the sale and distribution of the respective Tranche, including customary representations, warranties, covenants and indemnification provisions. For the avoidance of doubt, each Distribution Agreement shall include provisions to the following effect: (i) GWG shall have no responsibility for the payment of fees or commissions payable to the Bank, which fees and commissions shall be the responsibility of certain affiliates of the Seller Trusts as set forth in the applicable Distribution Agreement; and (ii) each of GWG and the Seller Trusts shall agree to provide a customary indemnity in favor of the Bank and its affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Bank' with 'advisory firm' in section 1.5. Section 1.6, the contradicted text, explicitly refers to 'the Bank' and outlines responsibilities related to fees and indemnification. By removing the Bank as the primary actor in section 1.5, the subsequent references to 'the Bank' in section 1.6 become inconsistent and ambiguous. It creates confusion about which entity the section refers to, causing a direct conflict in defining the parties' responsibilities and the applicability of the agreement terms. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, adhering to the Omissions - In Text Contradiction type because the entity 'Bank' was removed as a key term that is later required and referenced.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion. - 4 - (b) The Engagement Letter shall provide that the Bank may terminate its engagement at any time upon not less than 45 days' prior written notice to the other Parties. In the event the Bank provides such notice at any time prior to the sale of greater than 50% of the Shares and within 12 months of the Effective Date, then GWG and the Trust Advisors may elect (A) to terminate this OMA or (B) to continue this OMA and to seek to appoint a substitute investment bank, in which case GWG, after consultation with the Trust Advisors, shall be entitled to appoint a substitute investment bank to serve as the lead joint-book-running manager for the sale of the Shares. In the event the Bank terminates the Engagement Letter at any time following the sale of greater than 50% of the Shares and within 12 months of the Effective Date, the termination thereof shall constitute a concurrent Termination of this OMA.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Advisory Firm. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion. - 4 - (b) The Engagement Letter shall provide that the advisory firm may terminate its engagement at any time. In the event the advisory firm provides such notice, then GWG and the Trust Advisors may elect to terminate this OMA or continue this OMA and appoint a substitute advisory firm.
perturbation.explanation: This change simplifies the termination clause related to the advisory firm. The removed portion specifies the consequences of the Bank terminating its engagement at different stages of the share sale (before or after 50% of shares are sold, within 12 months of the effective date). By removing these specific conditions, the contract loses clarity on how the termination of the advisory firm affects the overall agreement. The explicit mention of the "Bank" in the original text is replaced by "advisory firm," but the subsequent clause regarding the Bank's termination and its implications is omitted, leading to a potential in-text contradiction when considering other sections that still implicitly rely on the consequences described in the removed clause. For example, if the substituted firm isn't another bank, then it creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 The Seller Trusts and GWG intend to retain one or more nationally recognized bulge bracket investment banks (the "Bank") for the marketing and resale of Shares pursuant to a separate agreement (the "Engagement Letter") to advise them in connection with the sale of the Tranches (together, the "Offerings"). The services to be performed by the advisory firm will be outlined in a separate document. The advisory firm will provide advice as needed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits crucial details regarding the consequences of the advisory firm's termination, specifically concerning the timing of the share sale and the 12-month period after the effective date. This omission contradicts the original intent, as the contradicted text (1.4) refers to the 'Bank,' implying a specific type of institution providing financial advisory services in connection with the sale of shares. The removal of details on how the BANK's termination affects the OMA, particularly in relation to the percentage of shares sold, creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction. If a non-bank advisory firm is appointed, it is unclear whether it falls under the same terms as the BANK. This introduces uncertainty as to the obligations and rights of each party related to termination, directly conflicting with the implicit assumptions of 1.4.
